steps.title: Identify the midsegment
steps.content: Since $M$ is the midpoint of $PQ$ and $N$ is the midpoint of $QR$, segment $MN$ is a midsegment of triangle $PQR$. The midsegment connects two side midpoints, is parallel to the third side, and is half its length.

steps.title: Double the midsegment
steps.content: Because $MN=15$, double the midsegment length to get the third side: $$ PR=2\cdot 15=30 $$ The answer is A.
